pocket_id_cutover.title: Retire pocket-id: stand up CuraOS-native OIDC/OAuth2 provider in identity-service and switch the broker to our own issuer
pocket_id_cutover.current_state_summary: identity-service is a strong AUTHENTICATION service (login, password+argon2+HIBP, WebAuthn/passkey, DPoP, refresh-sessions in Valkey+PG, break-glass, MFA factors, recovery codes, consents, data-export/deletion self-service, RBAC admin) that signs ES256 first-party CuraOS tokens. It is NOT an OIDC/OAuth2 PROVIDER: no /authorize, no /.well-known/openid-configuration, no exposed JWKS endpoint, no OAuth client registry, no consent-screen flow, no /token grant_type machinery, no /userinfo, no introspection/revocation. The 19-22 FE apps log in against pocket-id (auth.example.com) and the broker (src/auth/oidc-broker/*) only VALIDATES pocket-id id_tokens and re-mints CuraOS tokens. pocket-id is therefore the relying-party-facing OIDC PROVIDER we must replace; the broker is the bridge we must repoint at our own provider then delete.
pocket_id_cutover.recommended_provider_core: node-oidc-provider (Logto's fork choice; pure TS, fits Bun+NestJS) wrapped as a NestJS module inside identity-service, backed by a Drizzle/Postgres adapter (Citus tenant-scoped via nid column). Logto is the closest stack-fit corpus: mine packages/core/src/oidc/* (adapter.ts, grants/*, scope.ts, extra-token-claims.ts, resource.ts, init.ts) as the port-adapt template. Ory-Hydra-as-service is the fallback if certification risk demands it (it is the cleanest 'no user management, headless provider' separation - identity-service already speaks its challenge/verifier pattern via login/consent accept), but it is a Go service-boundary add that breaks the single-stack/single-binary Bun-compile charter, so it is the alternative-in-ADR, not the default.

parity_checklist.capability: OIDC discovery (/.well-known/openid-configuration) + JWKS endpoint (/.well-known/jwks.json)
parity_checklist.evidence: ES256 key handling + JWKS-capable key material exists in login-token.service.ts (importJWK/exportJWK/calculateJwkThumbprint, kid in protected header) but NO public discovery or JWKS HTTP endpoint is exposed; platform-bearer-verifier reads a static CURAOS_JWT_ES256_PUBLIC_JWK env, not a published JWKS.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Add provider well-known + JWKS routes via node-oidc-provider; expose the existing ES256 keyset; add key-rotation window. Generator-first: emit the discovery/JWKS contract in auth.tsp.

parity_checklist.capability: Authorization endpoint (/authorize) - code flow + PKCE (mandatory for public), id_token response types
parity_checklist.evidence: No /authorize route anywhere in src/ (grep confirmed); login is a direct POST /auth/login returning tokens, not a browser authorization redirect flow.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Add node-oidc-provider authorization handler; bridge its interaction (login+consent) to the existing login-user.service + webauthn + MFA. hosted-login renders the interaction pages.

parity_checklist.capability: Token endpoint (/token) - authorization_code, refresh_token, device_code, client_credentials
parity_checklist.evidence: Token MINTING (ES256, DPoP cnf binding, refresh-session rotation) is fully built in login-token.service.ts + refresh-session.service.ts, but it is NOT exposed as an OAuth /token endpoint with grant_type dispatch; refresh is a bespoke POST /auth/refresh.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Wire node-oidc-provider token grants to reuse the existing token-signing + refresh-session store as the adapter backend. Add device_code + client_credentials grants. Keep DPoP.

parity_checklist.capability: Userinfo endpoint + token introspection (RFC 7662) + revocation (RFC 7009)
parity_checklist.evidence: No /userinfo, /introspect, /revoke routes. Account profile exists at /account/profile but is not the OIDC userinfo claim shape.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: node-oidc-provider provides all three; map claims from existing user model + @curaos/policy roles via a claims/scope mapper (mine Logto scope.ts + extra-token-claims.ts).

parity_checklist.capability: End-session / RP-initiated logout (+ front/back-channel logout)
parity_checklist.evidence: POST /auth/logout + session revocation exist but as first-party session kill, not OIDC end_session with id_token_hint or back-channel logout to clients.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Add OIDC end-session handler; cascade to refresh-session revocation; add backchannel_logout_uri per client.

parity_checklist.capability: OIDC client (relying-party) management - CRUD, secret rotation, PKCE flag, callback/logout URLs, skip-consent, group restriction
parity_checklist.evidence: No OidcClient model, no /oidc/clients routes, no client secret hashing. admin.controller.ts only manages users + roles.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: New oidc_clients table + CRUD admin routes + secret hashing (argon2, reuse password-hasher). Seed the 19-22 FE apps + the broker consumer as clients. Generator-first: emit per-client registration into the contract.

parity_checklist.capability: Device authorization flow (RFC 8628) - device_code + user_code + verification UI
parity_checklist.evidence: No device endpoints.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: node-oidc-provider device flow + a verification page in hosted-login.

parity_checklist.capability: PAR - pushed authorization requests
parity_checklist.evidence: No PAR endpoint.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Enable node-oidc-provider PAR feature.

parity_checklist.capability: Federated client authentication (private_key_jwt / client_assertion JWT bearer + JWKS cache + JTI replay)
parity_checklist.evidence: JTI replay infrastructure exists (dpop-replay-store.ts); JWKS-fetch + verify exists in oidc-broker (createRemoteJWKSet + jwtVerify). No client_assertion auth method.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: node-oidc-provider supports client_secret_jwt + private_key_jwt natively; reuse dpop-replay-store pattern for client-assertion JTI dedup.

parity_checklist.capability: WebAuthn/Passkey registration + login + credential mgmt (list/rename/delete) + device state
parity_checklist.evidence: Full implementation: webauthn-registration.service.ts, webauthn-authentication.service.ts, webauthn-policy.service.ts, credential routes (register/options+verify, authenticate/options+verify, recovery, delete) in auth.controller.ts.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: None for primitives. Surface passkey as an interaction step inside the new /authorize flow (currently only reachable via direct POST). Add rename/label (pocket-id has it).

parity_checklist.capability: User model: username, email(optional), names, displayName, locale, admin flag, disabled, ldapId, custom claims, profile picture
parity_checklist.evidence: User model + roles + account profile + consents exist; custom-claims (per-user/per-group KV injected into tokens), ldapId, profile-picture upload are not confirmed present.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Add custom_claims table (per-user + per-group) + claims mapper; add profile-picture upload (route to @curaos/storage); add ldapId column for federation linkage.

parity_checklist.capability: User groups (membership, per-group custom claims, group-gated client access, ldapId)
parity_checklist.evidence: RBAC roles exist (rbac-policy.service.ts, role assign/revoke) but pocket-id 'user groups' (coarse membership + allowedOidcClients gating + per-group claims) is a distinct concept not modeled.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Model groups as either RBAC role-groups or B2B org-roles; add allowed_user_groups gate on oidc_clients. Reshape to org membership (see capability_map org model).

parity_checklist.capability: One-time access tokens (admin + self-service, email delivery) + signup tokens (time/usage-limited, default group/claims)
parity_checklist.evidence: Registration tokens exist (registration-tenant-resolver.ts, registration-store.ts 29KB) and tenant-scoped registration; password-reset + recovery-codes in account.controller.ts. pocket-id signup-token usage-limit/usage-count + one-time-access-email request-while-unauthenticated may be incomplete.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Extend registration tokens with usageLimit/usageCount + default-claims; add unauthenticated one-time-access-email request endpoint + email delivery via @curaos/providers EmailProvider.

parity_checklist.capability: Email verification (send + verify + emailVerified flag)
parity_checklist.evidence: Account self-service surface is broad but a dedicated send-verification/verify-email + emailVerified flag is not confirmed in routes (account has password-reset, not email-verify).
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Add email-verification token + send/verify routes (EmailProvider) + emailVerified column surfaced as a claim.

parity_checklist.capability: Initial admin setup (passwordless/passkey-based bootstrap)
parity_checklist.evidence: break-glass + seeded operator->alpha tenant tenant-admin default in broker config; a first-run passkey admin-setup flow is not explicit.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Add a one-shot /setup that registers the first admin via passkey, then locks; reuse break-glass audit.

parity_checklist.capability: API keys (personal access tokens) - scoped, expiry, lastUsedAt, expiration email
parity_checklist.evidence: No api-keys table/routes; platform-bearer-verifier is for service tokens not user PATs.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Add api_keys table + CRUD + middleware (mine pocket-id apikey/* and supertokens). client_credentials grant may cover machine-to-machine; user PAT still needed.

parity_checklist.capability: Audit logging (event, user, UA, IP, geo, JSON data) + query API
parity_checklist.evidence: Rich audit: identity-core/audit/*, auth-audit-publisher.ts (14KB), tamper-evident hash-chain per architecture_notes, event-led outbox. Geo enrichment (GeoLite2) is the only pocket-id extra not confirmed.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Add optional geo enrichment (provider); ensure OIDC provider events (authorize/consent/token) feed the existing audit chain.

parity_checklist.capability: LDAP sync (users/groups/membership, attribute mapping, soft-delete, admin-group detection)
parity_checklist.evidence: No LDAP code; ldapId column not present.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: See gaps: new federation adapter (mine authentik sources/ldap + keycloak federation/ldap as reference). Polling sync via scheduler + @curaos/providers.

parity_checklist.capability: SCIM service-provider config + sync trigger
parity_checklist.evidence: No SCIM. pocket-id itself is only an integration point (no full SCIM API).
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Defer to v1.1; full SCIM 2.0 (mine zitadel internal/api/scim + keycloak scim/* for full CRUD). pocket-id parity needs only the config+trigger stub.

parity_checklist.capability: Sessions (OAuth2 session store: codes/tokens/device/PAR + interaction sessions + 30-day refresh)
parity_checklist.evidence: refresh-session.service.ts + valkey-refresh-session-store.ts (21KB) + refresh-session-store.ts (13KB) are production-grade for refresh sessions; OAuth2 code/interaction session store does not exist (no provider).
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: node-oidc-provider Drizzle adapter persists codes/grants/interaction/device/PAR; reuse existing refresh-session store as the refresh-token backend so revocation/listing stays unified.

parity_checklist.capability: App configuration (40+ KV vars: SMTP, signup, feature toggles, branding)
parity_checklist.evidence: Unleash feature flags (tenancy), @curaos/secrets for secrets, @curaos/providers for SMTP. A unified per-tenant app-config KV store is not modeled.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Add per-tenant oidc/sign-in-experience config (mine Logto sign-in-experience). Branding -> theming.

parity_checklist.capability: Admin console (clients, users, groups, claims, app-config, audit viewer)
parity_checklist.evidence: admin.controller.ts covers users+roles only; hosted-login has account/settings UI. No client-mgmt/audit-viewer admin UI.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Extend admin API + a CuraOS admin app (generator emitUiApp) for clients/groups/claims/audit. Person+tenant-centric: org-admin scope, not global.

parity_checklist.capability: Account self-service console (profile, passkeys, authorized-clients view/revoke, API keys, email verify, one-time access)
parity_checklist.evidence: account.controller.ts is the broadest surface in the repo (profile, mfa, password-reset, recovery-codes, sessions, trusted-devices, consents, data-export, deletion-request, security-overview, recommendations, activity) + hosted-login UI.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Add authorized-OIDC-clients list + revoke (needs provider grant store) + API-keys panel + email-verify. Already exceeds pocket-id on GDPR (data-export/deletion).

parity_checklist.capability: Login/signup/consent/device UI flow
parity_checklist.evidence: hosted-login renders sign-in/account/settings (real-working) but NO consent screen, NO authorize-interaction page, NO device-verification page (no provider to drive them).
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Add interaction routes in hosted-login driven by node-oidc-provider interaction API: consent, account-selection, reauth, device.

parity_checklist.capability: Rate limiting (login, one-time-access, webauthn verify, signup)
parity_checklist.evidence: login-lockout.service.ts + login-lockout-store.ts; webauthn login finish rate-limited per pocket-id parity intent.
parity_checklist.work_to_parity: Extend lockout to new authorize/token/one-time-access endpoints.

cutover_steps.name: Author OIDC provider contracts (generator-first)
cutover_steps.detail: Extend specs/auth.tsp + auth.asyncapi.yaml with the provider surface: discovery, JWKS, /authorize, /token (grant_type union), /userinfo, /introspect, /revoke, /end-session, /device, /par, and oidc_clients CRUD. Regenerate @curaos/identity-sdk. This is where the feature lands FIRST per generator-evolution-rule.
cutover_steps.gate: tsp compiles, SDK regenerates, contract review

cutover_steps.name: Stand up the provider module behind a flag
cutover_steps.detail: Add a NestJS oidc-provider module wrapping node-oidc-provider with a Drizzle adapter (nid/tenant-scoped). Reuse existing ES256 keyset (login-token.service), refresh-session store (refresh-token backend), DPoP, webauthn + MFA + lockout as the interaction implementations. Port-adapt Logto packages/core/src/oidc/* (adapter, grants, scope, extra-token-claims). Ship dark (feature flag CURAOS_OWN_OIDC=off).
cutover_steps.gate: provider boots; discovery+JWKS serve; conformance smoke (authorization_code+PKCE round-trip) green locally

cutover_steps.name: Build interaction UI in hosted-login
cutover_steps.detail: Add authorize-interaction, consent, account-selection, reauth, and device-verification pages driven by the provider interaction API. Wire to existing login/webauthn/MFA. Person-centric: consent screen shows the person exactly what each client gets and lets them revoke later.
cutover_steps.gate: full browser code-flow login through hosted-login works locally with real data

cutover_steps.name: Register clients + migrate
cutover_steps.detail: Seed oidc_clients for the 19-22 FE apps + the broker consumer (same client_ids/redirect_uris pocket-id used so nothing changes for the apps). Migrate users: pocket-id users are already mapped into CuraOS actors by the broker's deterministic UUIDv5 (deriveActorId) keyed on subject - keep that derivation so existing actor rows + sessions survive. Migrate passkeys: export pocket-id WebauthnCredential rows (credentialId, publicKey, transports, backup state) into the existing webauthn-registration store schema (compatible shape). Migrate groups/claims into the new group/claims model.
cutover_steps.gate: every existing user resolves to the same actor id; every registered passkey verifies against our provider; client list complete

cutover_steps.name: Switch the broker to our own issuer
cutover_steps.detail: Repoint oidc-broker-config idpIssuer/idpAudience/JWKS at OUR provider (curaos-identity issuer) instead of auth.example.com/pocket-id. The broker keeps working unchanged because it just validates id_tokens against a discovery JWKS - now ours. This is the seam that makes cutover reversible: flip issuer back to pocket-id to roll back.
cutover_steps.gate: broker validates our id_tokens; FE apps log in end-to-end against our provider; live verification on a canary tenant

cutover_steps.name: Collapse the broker (optional, post-stable)
cutover_steps.detail: Once apps log in directly against our provider, the broker becomes a no-op (our id_token IS already a CuraOS token shape, or the token endpoint mints the downstream shape directly). Delete src/auth/oidc-broker/* and the /auth/oidc/exchange route, or keep it solely for genuine BYO-external-OIDC federation (its real long-term purpose).
cutover_steps.gate: no app depends on the broker for first-party login; broker repurposed to external-IdP-only

cutover_steps.name: Decommission pocket-id
cutover_steps.detail: Drain auth.example.com traffic, snapshot pocket-id DB for audit retention, tear down the pocket-id deployment + DNS, remove CURAOS_OIDC_* pocket-id env. Keep the snapshot per audit-retention policy.
cutover_steps.gate: zero traffic to pocket-id for the soak window; rollback issuer-flip proven before teardown; user-confirmed (destructive)

pocket_id_cutover.license_note: pocket-id is BSD-2-Clause (permissive, vendor-safe) but it is Go+Svelte+Fosite+GORM - wrong stack. We port-adapt PATTERNS (data model, endpoint shapes, claims), not code. node-oidc-provider (MIT via Logto's fork pattern) is the reuse vehicle on our TS stack.

best_sources: Logto packages/core/src/oidc/* (init.ts, adapter.ts, grants/*, scope.ts, extra-token-claims.ts, resource.ts) - closest stack-fit, MIT
node-oidc-provider (Logto's underlying fork) - the actual lib
ory-hydra /oauth2 + /consent + Fosite - certified reference for grant/flow correctness
pocket-id /backend/internal/oidc/* - lean BSD reference for the minimal endpoint set + data model
capability_map.integration_mode: port-adapt Logto patterns; vendor node-oidc-provider (MIT) as the dependency; reference Hydra/pocket-id for correctness. NOT service-boundary (keep single Bun stack).
capability_map.generator_first_target: auth.tsp + auth.asyncapi.yaml gain the full provider surface; @curaos/identity-sdk regenerated; emitServiceLive wires the module; oidc_clients registration becomes a contract-driven seed.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: Provider is tenant-scoped via nid column (Citus). Consent screen is person-centric: the person sees exactly what each client receives and can revoke from the account console. Issuer/JWKS are per-deployment, air-gap viable (no external discovery calls).
capability_map.license_note: Logto MPL-2.0 (service-boundary safe, we take patterns not files); node-oidc-provider MIT; Hydra/pocket-id Apache-2.0/BSD-2 reference.

best_sources: Logto packages/core/src/saml-application + connector-saml (TS, MPL-2.0, closest stack)
capability_map.integration_mode: port-adapt from Logto TS SAML; reference Keycloak for assertion/signature/SLO correctness. Vendor a TS SAML lib (e.g. node-saml/samlify family) rather than build XML-DSig.
capability_map.generator_first_target: saml_application + saml_connector contracts in TypeSpec; provider/connector config emitted per-tenant.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: SAML is a per-org SSO source (B2B): each org configures its own IdP; person federates in and lands in their org context. Defer to v1.1.

best_sources: Logto @simplewebauthn/server usage (same lib family we use)
pocket-id go-webauthn data model for credential-rename/device-state
capability_map.integration_mode: extend local; surface passkey as an interaction step in the new /authorize flow; add label/rename.
capability_map.generator_first_target: credential-management routes already in auth.tsp; add rename op.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: Already person-owned credentials. Add per-device naming + last-used so the person manages their own keys; admin surface is read-only for support.

capability_map.area: MFA (TOTP, SMS/email OTP, recovery codes, step-up)
capability_map.local_owner: identity-service (account/mfa + new step-up in authorize flow)
capability_map.maturity: partial (MFA factors CRUD + recovery codes exist; TOTP/SMS/email OTP + AAL step-up not confirmed)
capability_map.integration_mode: extend local; port-adapt Kratos AAL session model for step-up; use @curaos/providers for SMS/email delivery.
capability_map.generator_first_target: mfa factor types + AAL claim (amr/acr) in contract; challenge ops emitted.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: Person chooses+manages their factors in account console; org policy can REQUIRE a minimum AAL but the person keeps control of which methods. Tenant policy via @curaos/policy.
capability_map.license_note: Kratos/Zitadel Apache-2.0/AGPL (Zitadel patterns only, no code); Logto MPL pattern.

best_sources: Logto connector-kit + connectors/* (50+ TS connectors, MPL-2.0 - DIRECT pattern fit)
capability_map.maturity: partial (broker already validates external OIDC tokens + JWKS fetch + JTI replay)
capability_map.integration_mode: port-adapt Logto connector-kit abstract-class pattern; the existing broker is exactly the seam - keep it for BYO-external-OIDC after first-party cutover.
capability_map.generator_first_target: connector contract + per-connector config; provider abstraction via @curaos/providers.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: Person can link/unlink external identities to their CuraOS account (account console). Org can pin which providers its members may use. Account-linking by email matches the broker's deriveActorId stability.

capability_map.integration_mode: reference Keycloak for attribute-mapping/sync semantics; build a TS polling-sync adapter (vendor ldapjs). Self-hosted-first: customer LDAP, no cloud directory.
capability_map.generator_first_target: ldap_source config contract + sync-trigger op; ldapId column added to user model.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: LDAP is a per-org directory source for B2B tenants; synced users land in the org with org-scoped roles. Soft-delete = disable not remove (person data preserved).
capability_map.license_note: Keycloak Apache-2.0; authentik MIT; pocket-id BSD-2.

best_sources: supertokens AuthRecipe primary-user + account-linking (multiple credentials -> one primary)
capability_map.maturity: partial (user+roles+rich self-service exist; custom-claims, profile-picture, machine accounts, account-linking, lifecycle states incomplete)
capability_map.integration_mode: extend local; port-adapt supertokens account-linking + Zitadel lifecycle states + Kratos trait-schema for extensible attributes.
capability_map.generator_first_target: user model + custom_claims + machine-account in contract; lifecycle state enum; claims-mapper.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: One PERSON, many identities (password+passkey+social+enterprise) linked to one primary actor - the broker's deterministic UUIDv5 already implements this stability. Person owns + exports + deletes their data (GDPR self-service already exceeds pocket-id).

best_sources: Zitadel Instance->Org->Project hierarchy (the canonical 3-level B2B model)
Keycloak Organizations feature (org-within-realm, invitations, domain discovery)
Logto Organizations + OrganizationRoles + OrganizationScopes + OrganizationInvitations + email-domain JIT SSO (TS, closest fit)
capability_map.local_owner: @curaos/tenancy (deployment tenant) + NEW B2B org layer in identity-service (org-within-tenant)
capability_map.maturity: partial (tenancy = deployment-tenant isolation is real-working; B2B org-within-tenant is absent)
capability_map.integration_mode: port-adapt Logto's org model (it is our exact stack + the WorkOS-superset shape the scope demands). Map: @curaos/tenancy stays the DEPLOYMENT/isolation tenant (Citus shared/schema/db-per-tenant); add Logto-style Organizations as customer-orgs WITHIN a tenant, with org roles/scopes/invitations/JIT-domain SSO.
capability_map.generator_first_target: organization + org_role + org_scope + org_invitation + email_domain contracts; org claims (org_id, org_roles) injected into tokens via the claims mapper.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: Two layers, both honored: deployment-tenant = isolation boundary (PHI never crosses); customer-org = the B2B grouping the PERSON belongs to. A person can belong to multiple orgs; org-switch is a person-centric account action. JIT email-domain join makes onboarding a person-journey, not an admin task.
capability_map.license_note: Logto MPL-2.0 (pattern reuse, our stack); Zitadel AGPL + Keycloak Apache-2.0 (reference only).

best_sources: Logto roles + org-roles + role-scope + token claim injection
capability_map.maturity: partial (engine real but scaffold-only package; org-roles + composite roles + scope-claims absent)
capability_map.integration_mode: extend @curaos/policy; port-adapt Logto org-role/scope -> token-claim path + Keycloak composite-roles.
capability_map.generator_first_target: role + scope + role-scope + composite contracts; role claims in token mapper.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: Roles are org-scoped (B2B): a person has different roles in different orgs. Token carries org_roles for the active org. Person-facing apps see capabilities, not raw roles.
capability_map.license_note: @curaos/policy is ours (clean-room, ADR-noted no GPL/AGPL/MPL lift); references Apache/MPL patterns only.

best_sources: OpenFGA contextual tuples + CEL conditions (ABAC overlaid on ReBAC, Apache-2.0)
capability_map.maturity: partial (two conditions + additive model; no attribute-expression/CEL engine)
capability_map.integration_mode: extend @curaos/policy; for richer attribute rules, port-adapt OpenFGA CEL-condition evaluation rather than build a DSL.
capability_map.generator_first_target: abac_rule contracts; condition catalog; field-permission masks already modeled.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: ABAC is the person-protective layer: subjectIsOwner gives the person self-access by default; tenantMatch prevents cross-tenant leakage. Additive-only means policy never silently revokes a person's own-data access.

best_sources: OpenFGA (Zanzibar: model DSL, Check/ListObjects/ListUsers/Expand, contextual tuples, weighted-graph opt, Postgres backend, Apache-2.0)
capability_map.local_owner: NEW pkg @curaos/entitlements (ReBAC service) OR @curaos/policy extension; consumed by identity-service for client/scope gating
capability_map.integration_mode: DECISION POINT (see decisions): OpenFGA-as-embedded-Go-service (best engine, breaks single-stack) vs Keto (same, Connect-RPC) vs extend @curaos/policy with a relation-tuple table on Postgres (single-stack, less optimized). Given Citus + single-Bun-stack charter, lean to port-adapt the Zanzibar tuple model + Check algorithm INTO @curaos/policy on Postgres; OpenFGA as the reference + the escape hatch if Check latency demands it.
capability_map.generator_first_target: authorization_model DSL + relation_tuple + Check/ListObjects contracts; tuples are tenant-scoped (nid).
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: Entitlements answer 'can THIS person do THIS to THIS resource' - the core person-centric authz question. Resource ownership tuples make the person the owner of their records by default; sharing is an explicit person-driven grant.
capability_map.license_note: OpenFGA + Keto Apache-2.0 (vendor or pattern); Keycloak Apache-2.0.

best_sources: ory-kratos flow state-machines + headless node/container model (Apache-2.0, the cleanest engine)
authentik Flow+Stage composition engine (21 stages, policy-gated, MIT)
Logto experience package + sign-in-experience policy engine (TS)
capability_map.maturity: partial (rich self-service routes exist but no declarative flow engine; flows are hardcoded)
capability_map.integration_mode: DECISION POINT: adopt a Kratos-style state-machine OR authentik-style stage-composition for configurable flows, OR keep hardcoded flows + node-oidc-provider interactions (lazy default). For v1, the provider's interaction API + existing services is enough; a declarative flow engine is v1.1 if tenants need custom flows.
capability_map.generator_first_target: flow/stage config contract (if adopted); otherwise interaction routes only.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: Flows are the person's journey (sign-in/recover/verify) - already the spine. Configurable per org branding. Recovery via code OR passkey keeps the person unlocked.

best_sources: Logto packages/console (React admin, MPL) + management REST
capability_map.local_owner: NEW frontend admin app (generator emitUiApp) + identity-service admin API
capability_map.maturity: partial (admin API = users+roles only; no client/group/claims/audit admin; no admin app)
capability_map.integration_mode: generator-first: emit a CuraOS admin app from the contract; reference Logto/Keycloak admin IA for what screens to include.
capability_map.generator_first_target: admin REST surface in contract; emitUiApp generates the admin app from @curaos/api-client hooks + @curaos/ui.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: Admin is ORG-admin-scoped, not god-mode: a customer-org admin manages only their org's members/roles/clients. Workflows organize around onboarding/serving the org's people, not raw CRUD.

best_sources: LOCAL account.controller + hosted-login (already best-in-class on GDPR)
capability_map.maturity: have (exceeds pocket-id: data-export, deletion-request, security-overview, recommendations, activity, trusted-devices)
capability_map.integration_mode: extend local; add authorized-clients view/revoke (needs provider grant store) + API-keys + email-verify + identity-linking.
capability_map.generator_first_target: account routes already contract-backed; add the 3 missing panels.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: This IS the person-centric surface - the person owns profile, credentials, sessions, consents, exports, deletion. Already the model the lens demands.

capability_map.maturity: absent (pocket-id itself only has config+trigger, no full API)
capability_map.integration_mode: build inbound SCIM 2.0 (Users+Groups CRUD) referencing Keycloak/Zitadel; outbound (sync to SaaS) v1.1. Defer to v1.1 overall.
capability_map.generator_first_target: SCIM resource schemas + endpoints in contract (it is just a REST surface over the user/group model).
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: SCIM provisions org members for B2B tenants - an IT-admin convenience that still results in person-owned accounts.

best_sources: ory-hydra challenge/verifier stateless flow + consent persistence
capability_map.maturity: partial (refresh sessions excellent; OAuth2 code/interaction/device/PAR session store absent until provider lands; theft-detection rotation not confirmed)
capability_map.integration_mode: extend local; provider Drizzle adapter persists code/interaction/device/PAR; reuse the refresh-session store as the refresh backend; port-adapt supertokens rotating-refresh theft detection.
capability_map.generator_first_target: session contracts (list/revoke already present); add theft-detection.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: Person sees+revokes every session/device in account console (already built). Per-org session lifespans (Kratos pattern) let stricter orgs shorten without affecting the person's other orgs.

best_sources: ory-hydra token hooks (AccessRequestHook/RefreshTokenHook HTTP callout) + Kratos webhook hooks
Zitadel Action flows (goja JS, pre/post auth/token)
capability_map.local_owner: identity-service (token-claims hook) + CuraOS workflow-core for richer flows
capability_map.maturity: absent (no auth hooks; but CuraOS has an event-led outbox + BPM engine to lean on)
capability_map.integration_mode: port-adapt Hydra token-hook for claims enrichment (resolve org/roles/tenant at token mint); for user-event side-effects, prefer the existing event-led outbox + workflow-core (charter: configure not code) over a bespoke webhook engine.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: Hooks enrich tokens with the person's active-org context; user-event flows (post-register onboarding) become person-journey BPM workflows.
capability_map.license_note: Hydra/Kratos Apache-2.0; Zitadel AGPL (pattern); Logto MPL.

best_sources: Zitadel event-sourced 100% audit trail (reference for completeness)
capability_map.integration_mode: extend local; feed provider events (authorize/consent/token/end-session) into the existing chain; add optional geo enrichment via provider.
capability_map.generator_first_target: audit-log query API + event schemas (already in audit-event.schema.ts).
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: Person can see their OWN security activity (account console activity/security-overview already built); org-admins see org-scoped audit; tamper-evidence protects the person's record.
capability_map.license_note: Zitadel AGPL (reference); authentik MIT; local is ours.

best_sources: LOCAL TypeSpec + AsyncAPI + @hey-api/openapi-ts SDK gen (generator-first spine)
capability_map.maturity: have (contracts-first is the established pattern; auth.tsp + auth.asyncapi.yaml exist)
capability_map.integration_mode: extend local contracts; every new provider/org/scim surface lands in TypeSpec FIRST then regenerates the SDK (generator-evolution-rule).
capability_map.generator_first_target: THIS IS the generator-first owner: all the above areas land as contract extensions + SDK regen + emitServiceLive/emitUiApp.
capability_map.person_tenant_reshape: Resource-oriented, tenant-scoped, person-readable. Same contract drives both person-facing and management surfaces.

gaps.absent_today: identity-service emits only first-party CuraOS tokens; no /authorize, discovery, JWKS endpoint, client registry, consent flow, /token grants, /userinfo, /introspect, /revoke, device, PAR.
gaps.best_source: node-oidc-provider (MIT) + Logto packages/core/src/oidc/* port-adapt + Hydra/pocket-id reference
steps: wrap node-oidc-provider as a NestJS module with a Drizzle/Citus adapter
reuse existing ES256 keyset + refresh-session store + DPoP + webauthn/MFA as interaction impls
register the 19-22 FE apps as clients
ship behind CURAOS_OWN_OIDC flag, conformance-smoke locally

gaps.absent_today: @curaos/tenancy is deployment-tenant isolation only; no customer-org grouping, org-roles, org-scopes, org-invitations, JIT email-domain SSO, multi-org membership.
gaps.best_source: Logto Organizations (exact stack) + Zitadel Instance->Org->Project + Keycloak Organizations
gaps.target_owner: new B2B org layer in identity-service + @curaos/policy (org-roles), tenancy stays the isolation boundary
steps: inject org_id+org_roles into token claims via the claims mapper
build org-switch + JIT-domain-join as person-centric account actions

gaps.absent_today: @curaos/policy is RBAC + additive ABAC + field-permlevels only; no relation tuples, no Check/ListObjects/ListUsers/Expand, no resource-ownership graph.
gaps.target_owner: @curaos/policy extension (Postgres relation-tuple table + Check) OR new @curaos/entitlements; OpenFGA as escape-hatch service
steps: port-adapt Zanzibar tuple model + Check algorithm into @curaos/policy on Citus (single-stack default)
benchmark Check latency; promote to embedded OpenFGA only if needed
gaps.version: v1.1 (RBAC+ABAC suffice for v1; entitlements is the superset push)

gaps.absent_today: no api_keys table/routes; only service-token verification.
steps: expiry + lastUsedAt + expiration email via EmailProvider

gaps.absent_today: no LDAP adapter, no ldapId column, no sync.

steps: vendor a TS SAML lib (samlify family) for XML-DSig

gaps.absent_today: no SCIM (pocket-id itself only has config+trigger stub).
steps: inbound SCIM Users+Groups CRUD over existing user/org model

gaps.absent_today: broker validates external tokens but there is no connector framework / linkable social identities / per-org provider pinning.
gaps.version: v1 (broker exists; productize) / v1.1 (full connector catalog)

gaps.gap: Email verification + signup-token usage limits + unauthenticated one-time-access-email
gaps.absent_today: password-reset + recovery-codes exist; explicit email-verify flow + emailVerified claim + signup-token usageLimit/usageCount + unauth one-time-access request not confirmed.
steps: email-verification token + send/verify routes + emailVerified claim
extend registration tokens with usage limits + default claims

gaps.absent_today: custom_claims, profile-picture upload, machine (service) accounts, account-linking, lifecycle-state enum not confirmed.
gaps.best_source: pocket-id CustomClaim + Zitadel Machine accounts + supertokens account-linking

gaps.gap: Provider interaction UI (consent, account-selection, reauth, device-verify) + admin app for clients/groups/claims/audit

steps: interaction routes in hosted-login driven by provider interaction API
emitUiApp the admin app from the admin contract

gaps.gap: Declarative self-service flow engine (configurable per-org flows)
gaps.absent_today: flows are hardcoded in services; no Kratos-style state machine or authentik-style stage composition.

v1.1 adopt a flow engine only if tenants demand custom flows

decisions.question: What library/strategy backs our OWN OIDC/OAuth2 provider?

options.choice: node-oidc-provider wrapped in a NestJS module (Logto's choice)
pros: pure TS, fits Bun+NestJS single-stack + Bun-compile single-binary charter
Logto is a proven production reference on this exact lib (port-adapt packages/core/src/oidc/*)

cons: violates ponytail/YAGNI - rung 5 says use the installed/available lib

options.choice: Ory Hydra as a separate Go service
pros: identity-service already speaks its challenge/verifier pattern
cons: Go service-boundary breaks the single Bun stack + single-binary compile charter
Apache-2.0 fine but adds a second runtime

decisions.recommendation: node-oidc-provider in NestJS (Logto-aligned). Hydra is the documented escape-hatch if certification pressure outweighs single-stack. Build-from-scratch rejected.
binding_constraints: Logto weighted as closest stack-fit reuse

decisions.question: How do we deliver fine-grained entitlements (ReBAC)?

options.choice: Port-adapt Zanzibar tuple model + Check INTO @curaos/policy on Citus/Postgres
cons: less optimized than OpenFGA's weighted-graph Check

options.choice: Embed OpenFGA as a Go service

decisions.recommendation: Extend @curaos/policy with a Postgres relation-tuple table + Check (port-adapt OpenFGA model/algorithm) for v1.1; promote to embedded OpenFGA ONLY if Check latency benchmarks fail. RBAC+ABAC already cover v1.
binding_constraints: no GPL/AGPL/MPL code lift (Apache-2.0 OpenFGA pattern OK)

decisions.question: How do we model B2B organizations vs deployment tenants?

options.choice: Two layers: @curaos/tenancy = deployment/isolation tenant (Citus); Logto-style Organizations = customer-org WITHIN a tenant
pros: preserves PHI isolation boundary (tenant) while adding B2B grouping (org)
Logto org model is our exact stack + the WorkOS-superset shape required
person can belong to multiple orgs
cons: two concepts to keep distinct in docs/UX

decisions.recommendation: Two-layer: keep @curaos/tenancy as the isolation boundary; add Logto-style Organizations as the B2B layer inside it. Org claims (org_id, org_roles) injected at token mint.
binding_constraints: PHI never crosses the deployment-tenant boundary

decisions.question: Do we adopt a declarative self-service flow engine?

options.choice: No engine for v1 - use node-oidc-provider interactions + existing hardcoded services
pros: YAGNI/ponytail - existing self-service is already broad
cons: per-org custom flows not configurable yet
large build for a need no tenant has yet

decisions.recommendation: v1: NO engine (provider interactions + existing services). Revisit in v1.1 ONLY if tenants demand custom flows. Lazy-ladder rung 1: question whether it needs to exist yet - it does not.

decisions.question: How do we federate external directories (LDAP/AD) and external IdPs?

options.choice: New @curaos/identity-federation pkg: TS LDAP polling-sync adapter (ldapjs) + repurpose oidc-broker for BYO-external-OIDC + Logto-connector-kit social connectors
pros: the broker ALREADY validates external OIDC + JWKS fetch + JTI replay - its real long-term purpose

options.choice: Embed Keycloak/authentik as the federation broker
cons: wrong stack, second runtime, breaks charter

decisions.recommendation: @curaos/identity-federation on our stack: keep+repurpose the existing broker for external-OIDC after first-party cutover; add LDAP (v1.1) + SAML (v1.1) adapters referencing Keycloak/authentik patterns. Social via Logto connector-kit pattern.
binding_constraints: broker is the existing federation seam - do not delete, repurpose
Logto MPL pattern reuse / Keycloak Apache reference

decisions.question: What is the reversible seam for the pocket-id cutover?

options.choice: Flip oidc-broker idpIssuer/JWKS from pocket-id to our provider
pros: broker is issuer-agnostic (validates any discovery JWKS) - one config flip
FE apps unaffected if client_ids/redirect_uris preserved
deriveActorId keeps every existing user mapped to the same actor
cons: broker stays in the path until apps move to direct login

options.choice: Hard-switch FE apps to our provider directly
cons: touches every FE app at once - high blast radius

decisions.recommendation: Phase via the broker issuer-flip (reversible canary), then move apps to direct login, then collapse/repurpose the broker, then decommission pocket-id. Decommission is destructive - user-confirm + soak window + DB snapshot first.
binding_constraints: verify-before-build (prove running behavior on canary tenant)
